name: Barbara Ann Halkier
title: Professor
summary: Professor Halkier is a world-leading expert in glucosinolate biosynthesis and transport, heading the DynaMo Center. Her work combines molecular biology, metabolic engineering, and synthetic biology to understand plant defense compounds. Strong relevance for computational modeling of metabolic networks, transporter identification, and pathway engineering.

name: Michael Broberg Palmgren
title: Professor
summary: Professor Palmgren leads the NovoCrops project on accelerated breeding of resilient crop plants. His research on membrane pumps and ion transport, combined with work on orphan crop domestication and gene editing, offers excellent opportunities for computational approaches to predict transporter function and crop improvement strategies.

name: Hussam Hassan Nour-Eldin
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor and Head of Section for Molecular Plant Biology. His research on transport engineering to eliminate toxic compounds from edible crop parts while maintaining plant fitness is highly relevant for bioinformatics approaches to transporter prediction and metabolic modeling.

name: Hans Thordal-Christensen
title: Professor
summary: Professor Thordal-Christensen studies molecular mechanisms of plant immunity against fungal pathogens, particularly powdery mildew. His work on effector-triggered immunity and membrane trafficking in defense responses provides opportunities for computational prediction of pathogen effectors and resistance mechanisms.

name: Staffan Persson
title: Professor in Plant Cell Biology, DNRF Chair, Villum Investigator, and NNF Laureate
topics: Plant cell wall biology and mechanics
Root responses to abiotic stress (soil compaction, salinity)
Transcriptional regulation of cell wall components
summary: Staffan Persson leads research on plant cell wall biology with particular focus on cellulose biosynthesis, the CESA complex, and how plants respond to environmental stresses through cell wall remodeling. He coordinates the Center for Advanced Bioimaging (CAB) and heads the Copenhagen Plant Science Center (CPSC), with joint appointments at Shanghai Jiao Tong University and University of Melbourne.
recentWork: Recent work includes a groundbreaking Nature publication (2025) on how ethylene modulates cell wall mechanics for root responses to soil compaction, revealing that plants use engineering principles (thick epidermis-soft cortex contrast) to penetrate compacted soil. Other recent publications include pupylation-based proximity labeling to identify cellulose biosynthesis regulatory factors (Nature Communications, 2025), phosphate starvation effects on cellulose synthesis, and comprehensive reviews on cellulose synthesis in land plants (Molecular Plant, 2023). Also published on salt stress responses in roots and callose-cellulose biosynthesis balance in plant immunity (2022-2024).
collaborationAngles: Computational modeling of cellulose synthase complex dynamics and regulation using AI/ML approaches to predict protein interactions and cell wall mechanics
Multi-omics integration (transcriptomics, proteomics, metabolomics) to understand cell wall biosynthesis regulatory networks under stress conditions
Evolutionary genomics of CESA gene families and cell wall synthesis pathways across plant species
Development of bioinformatics pipelines for proximity labeling data analysis (PUP-IT) to identify protein-protein interactions
Machine learning prediction of transcription factor binding sites regulating cellulose synthesis genes
Systems biology modeling of stress signaling pathways (ethylene, ABA, auxin) controlling cell wall remodeling
Comparative genomics approaches to identify conserved vs. species-specific cell wall stress response mechanisms
Image analysis and AI-based quantification of cellulose microfibril architecture and cell wall structure from advanced microscopy data

name: Søren Husted
title: Professor
summary: Professor Husted researches plant nutrient mobilization and uptake, with focus on nanofertilizers and nutrient use efficiency. His work on single-cell ionomics and multi-element profiling generates large datasets ideal for machine learning approaches to predict nutrient uptake patterns and optimize fertilization strategies.

name: Sander Bruun
title: Professor
summary: Professor Bruun leads research on climate change mitigation and sustainable soil management. His work on soil carbon dynamics, nitrogen cycling, and greenhouse gas emissions involves complex modeling and could benefit from AI/ML approaches for predicting soil processes and optimizing agricultural practices for climate mitigation.

name: Fulai Liu
title: Professor
summary: Professor Liu leads the Crop Stress Physiology group studying plant responses to drought and other abiotic stresses. His work on stress priming, cross-stress tolerance, and physiological mechanisms under climate change scenarios generates phenotypic data amenable to machine learning for stress prediction and crop improvement.

name: Thomas Georg Roitsch
title: Professor
summary: Professor Roitsch leads the Molecular Plant Physiology and Plant Phenomics group. His research on plant phenomics, combining physiological phenotyping with genomics, is highly relevant for AI/ML applications in image analysis, phenotype prediction, and bridging genotype-to-phenotype gaps in crop breeding.

name: David B. Collinge
title: Professor
summary: Professor Collinge leads the Plant Pathology and Microbiology group and heads the Section for Microbial Ecology and Biotechnology. His work on fungal endophytes for biological control and plant immunity involves genomics and transcriptomics approaches that could benefit from computational prediction of pathogen-plant interactions.

name: Nina Cedergreen
title: Professor
summary: Professor Cedergreen researches environmental toxicology with focus on pesticide effects and mixture toxicity. Her work involves dose-response modeling, benchmark dose estimation, and dynamic modeling of toxic effects - areas where machine learning and computational approaches for QSAR modeling and risk prediction could add value.

name: Meike Burow
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor Burow studies regulatory networks controlling glucosinolate metabolism. Her research on transcriptional regulation, natural variation, and genotype-to-phenotype relationships in plant defense chemistry is highly relevant for systems biology approaches and network modeling using computational methods.

name: Nicolai Vitt Meyling
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor Meyling heads the Insect-Microbe Interactions group, studying entomopathogenic fungi for biological control. His work on fungal genomics, host-pathogen interactions, and ecological applications of biocontrol agents could benefit from computational approaches for predicting fungal virulence and optimizing biocontrol strategies.

name: Kristian Thorup-Kristensen
title: Professor
summary: Professor Thorup-Kristensen leads research on crop root systems and belowground ecology. His work on root phenotyping in the RadiMax facility, combining minirhizotron imaging with machine learning for root-function relationships, offers excellent opportunities for AI-based image analysis and predictive modeling of root traits.

name: Jan H. Christensen
title: Professor
summary: Professor Christensen heads the Analytical Chemistry group and Research Centre for Advanced Analytical Chemistry (RAACE). His work on multidimensional chromatography, pixel-based analysis, and non-targeted screening generates massive datasets requiring advanced chemometrics and machine learning for data processing and pattern recognition.

name: Peter Ulvskov
title: Professor
summary: Professor Ulvskov leads the Cell Wall Biology and Bioengineering group within Plant Glycobiology. His research on cell wall polysaccharides, hemicelluloses, and glycosyltransferases involves structural biology and could benefit from computational prediction of enzyme function and cell wall architecture modeling.

name: Anders Albrechtsen
title: Professor
summary: Anders Albrechtsen is a Professor in the Computational and RNA Biology section specializing in developing statistical and computational methods for analyzing genomic data. His research focuses on population genetics, medical genetics, and method development for large-scale genomic datasets. He is highly relevant for collaborations in computational biology, genomics, and AI/ML applications to biological data.

name: Ida Moltke
title: Associate Professor
summary: Ida Moltke is an Associate Professor in the Computational and RNA Biology section developing and applying statistical and computational methods to genomic data for both population and medical genetics problems. Her work bridges computational methods with genetic analysis, making her an excellent collaborator for bioinformatics and computational biology projects.

name: Ole Winther
title: Professor
summary: Ole Winther is a Professor in Genomic Bioinformatics with joint appointments at DTU and UCPH. He leads research in machine learning and AI applications to biology and genomic medicine, heading the ELLIS Unit Copenhagen. His expertise in deep learning and bioinformatics makes him ideal for AI/ML collaborations in computational biology.

name: Robin Andersson
title: Associate Professor
summary: Robin Andersson is an Associate Professor and section head in the Computational and RNA Biology section. His research focuses on computational approaches to understanding gene regulation and RNA biology, making him relevant for genomics and bioinformatics collaborations.

name: Michael Poulsen
title: Professor
summary: Michael Poulsen is a Full Professor and Deputy Head of Department specializing in the evolution of microbial symbioses. His research on social and symbiotic evolution, particularly host-microbe interactions, provides opportunities for interdisciplinary collaboration combining evolutionary genomics with microbiology and bioinformatics.

name: Hans Henrik Bruun
title: Professor
summary: Hans Henrik Bruun is a Professor in the Ecology and Evolution section focusing on community ecology, biodiversity, and conservation science. His work on plant communities and ecosystem biodiversity could benefit from computational approaches and genomic tools for studying plant stress responses and adaptation.

name: Rasmus Kjøller
title: Associate Professor
summary: Rasmus Kjøller serves as Head of Studies for Biology (BSc) and works on mycorrhizal fungi and plant-microbe interactions. His research on fungal ecology and biodiversity intersects with molecular and genomic approaches, offering collaboration potential in plant science and stress biology.

name: Birthe B. Kragelund
title: Professor
summary: Birthe B. Kragelund is a Professor in the Biomolecular Sciences section specializing in protein structure-function relationships using NMR spectroscopy and biophysics. Her research on intrinsically disordered proteins and protein interactions could benefit from computational modeling and AI-based structure prediction methods.

name: Karen Skriver
title: Professor
summary: Karen Skriver is a Professor and Head of Studies for Biochemistry (MSc). Her research focuses on plant biochemistry and stress responses, making her highly relevant for collaborations in plant science, stress biology, and molecular approaches to understanding plant adaptation.

name: Jacobus (Koos) Boomsma
title: Professor
summary: Jacobus Boomsma is a Professor in the Ecology and Evolution section known for research on social evolution and ant genomics. His work with the Global Ant Genomics Alliance (GAGA) involves large-scale genomic analyses that could benefit from advanced computational and bioinformatics approaches.

name: Rasmus Hartmann-Petersen
title: Professor
summary: Rasmus Hartmann-Petersen is a Professor and Section Head for Biomolecular Sciences. His research on protein degradation and cellular quality control systems involves molecular and cell biology approaches that could be enhanced through computational modeling and systems biology approaches.

name: Søren J. Sørensen
title: Professor
summary: Søren J. Sørensen is a Professor and section head in Microbiology. His research on microbial communities and environmental microbiology could benefit from computational approaches for analyzing complex microbial datasets and metagenomic data.

name: Kim Rewitz
title: Professor
summary: Kim Rewitz is a Professor and section head for Cell Biology and Physiology. His research on developmental biology and endocrine regulation in insects involves molecular and genomic approaches that could integrate with computational biology methods.

name: Ole Pedersen
title: Professor
summary: Ole Pedersen is a Professor and section head specializing in plant physiology and stress adaptation. His work on how plants respond to environmental stress, particularly in aquatic environments, is highly relevant for plant science and stress biology collaborations.

name: Olaf Nielsen
title: Professor
summary: Olaf Nielsen is a Professor and section head for Functional Genomics. His research on genome stability, cell cycle regulation, and DNA repair uses model organisms and molecular approaches that could be enhanced through bioinformatics and computational genomics.

name: David Nash
title: Associate Professor
summary: David Nash is an Associate Professor and Section Head for Ecology and Evolution. His research on co-evolutionary dynamics of mutualistic and parasitic interactions could benefit from genomic and bioinformatic approaches to understand evolutionary processes.

name: Flemming Ekelund
title: Associate Professor
summary: Flemming Ekelund is an Associate Professor and section head working on soil and microbial ecology. His research on soil organisms and ecosystem functioning could integrate with molecular and metagenomic approaches for studying microbial communities.

name: Søren Tvorup Christensen
title: Professor
summary: Søren Tvorup Christensen is a Professor and section head for Cell and Neurobiology. His research on cell biology and signal transduction focuses on primary cilia and cellular communication mechanisms.

name: Anders Lydik Garm
title: Professor
summary: Anders Lydik Garm is a Professor and section head for Marine Biology. His research on sensory systems and neurobiology in marine invertebrates uses physiological and behavioral approaches.

name: Kaare Teilum
title: Professor
summary: Kaare Teilum is a Professor and Deputy Head of Department. His research uses NMR spectroscopy to study protein dynamics and structure, which could benefit from computational approaches and machine learning methods for structure prediction.

name: Dean Jacobsen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Dean Jacobsen is an Associate Professor and Deputy Head of Department working in freshwater biology. His research on aquatic ecosystems and biodiversity could integrate with genomic and molecular approaches.

name: Amelie Stein
title: Associate Professor
summary: Amelie Stein is an Associate Professor and Head of Studies for Bioinformatics. Her expertise in computational biology and structural bioinformatics makes her highly relevant for AI/ML collaborations in protein structure prediction and bioinformatics.

name: Jacob B. Hansen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Jacob B. Hansen is an Associate Professor and Head of Studies for Molecular Biomedicine. His work on genomics and gene regulation in medical contexts could benefit from advanced computational and bioinformatics approaches.

name: Lasse Riemann
title: Professor
summary: Lasse Riemann is a Professor in Marine Molecular Microbiology working on nitrogen fixation and microbial processes in marine ecosystems. His research uses molecular approaches that could integrate with metagenomic and bioinformatic analyses.

name: Katrine Worsaae
title: Professor
summary: Katrine Worsaae is a Professor and Deputy Head of Department for Teaching in the Marine Biology section. Her research on meiofauna genomics and animal evolution involves large-scale genomic projects that could benefit from bioinformatics and computational approaches.

name: Eske Willerslev
title: Professor
summary: Eske Willerslev is a Lundbeck Foundation Professor and Director of the Centre of Excellence in GeoGenetics, also holding a chair at Cambridge. His pioneering work in ancient DNA and evolutionary genomics involves massive genomic datasets that require advanced computational and bioinformatics approaches.

name: Mette Burmølle
title: Professor
summary: Mette Burmølle is a Professor in the Microbiology section studying bacterial interactions and biofilm formation. Her research on microbial communities could benefit from computational approaches for analyzing complex interaction networks and metagenomic data.

name: Henriette Pilegaard
title: Professor
summary: Henriette Pilegaard is a Professor and PhD coordinator for Cell Biology and Physiology, Genomics and Molecular Biomedicine. Her role involves coordinating research in these areas, which have strong computational components.

name: Anders Priemé
title: Associate Professor
summary: Anders Priemé is an Associate Professor and PhD coordinator for Microbiology, Ecology and Evolution. His work involves microbial ecology and evolutionary processes that could integrate with genomic and bioinformatic approaches.

name: Michael Lisby
title: Professor
summary: Michael Lisby is a Professor and PhD coordinator for Functional Genomics. His research on DNA repair and genome stability uses molecular and cell biology approaches that could benefit from computational genomics and systems biology methods.

name: Sine Lo Svenningsen
title: Professor
summary: Sine Lo Svenningsen is a Professor and PhD coordinator for Biomolecular Sciences, Cell and Neurobiology. Her research on bacterial regulation and molecular mechanisms could integrate with computational modeling approaches.

name: Niels Daugbjerg
title: Professor
summary: Niels Daugbjerg is a Professor and PhD coordinator for Freshwater Biology, Marine Biology, and Terrestrial Ecology. His research on algae and phycology could benefit from genomic and molecular approaches to study adaptation and diversity.

name: Poul Erik Jensen
title: Professor
summary: Professor in plant-based food biochemistry with over 25 years' experience in plant biochemistry, molecular biology and biotechnology. His research focuses on microalgae as sustainable protein sources, photosynthesis, and developing new plant-based foods. He previously discovered reverse photosynthesis and leads projects on rapeseed protein modification and algae-based ingredients. Strong relevance for computational biology through genomics work, plant science through photosynthesis and metabolism research, and potential for bioinformatics applications in protein design and metabolic engineering.

name: Lene Jespersen
title: Professor
summary: Professor in Microbial Ecology and Food Fermentation with research focusing on fermented foods, food mycology, yeast biodiversity, and microbial interactions. Her work includes single cell analysis, microbial functionality, food matrix interactions, and effects of food-borne microorganisms on gut microbiota and human health. Excellent collaboration potential for computational biology and bioinformatics in microbiome analysis, evolutionary genomics of yeast, and microbial community modeling.

name: Marianne Nissen Lund
title: Professor
summary: Professor heading the Food Proteins research group with joint appointment in Department of Food Science and Department of Biomedical Sciences. Research focuses on protein chemistry, modifications, and functionality in food systems including dairy and plant proteins. Her work on protein structure-function relationships and chemical modifications offers opportunities for computational modeling, proteomics, bioinformatics analysis of protein modifications, and structural biology collaborations.

name: Morten Arendt Rasmussen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor specializing in chemometrics and computational food and health science. His research applies machine learning and data analysis tools to food systems, including gut microbiome studies. Published high-impact work on antibiotic effects on gut microbiome recovery. Excellent fit for AI/ML, bioinformatics, and computational biology collaborations, particularly in microbiome research, multivariate data analysis, and predictive modeling of biological systems.

name: Lilia Ahrné
title: Professor
summary: Professor in Dairy Process Technology and Fellow of the International Academy of Food Science and Technology. Research focuses on sustainable process technologies, structure processing, and understanding effects of processing on physical, chemical and structural characteristics of foods. Collaboration potential in computational modeling of food processing, biophysical modeling, and process optimization using ML approaches.

name: Mogens Larsen Andersen
title: Professor
summary: Professor in Ingredient and Dairy Technology section. Research involves food chemistry and ingredient functionality. Potential for collaboration in computational chemistry, metabolomics, and data-driven approaches to understanding chemical reactions in food systems.

name: Marianne Thomsen
title: Professor
summary: Professor in Design and Consumer Behavior section, leading research on food waste management, sustainable food systems, and circular solutions. Her work on the FOODRUS project involves innovative approaches to reduce waste in supply chains. Potential collaboration in data science for consumer behavior analysis and sustainability metrics.

name: Wender Bredie
title: Professor
summary: Professor of Sensory Science researching taste biology, flavor perception, and consumer acceptance across cultures. Recent work uses AI methods to analyze fungiform papillae on tongues and understand genetic differences in taste perception. Collaboration opportunities in AI/ML for sensory data analysis, image analysis of biological structures, and computational modeling of taste perception.

name: Nils Arneborg
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor in Food Microbiology, Gut Health, and Fermentation section. Head of Studies for BSc Food and Nutrition. Research focuses on yeast biology, fermentation processes, and microbial interactions in food systems. Potential for bioinformatics and genomics collaborations in yeast strain analysis and microbial community dynamics.

name: Dennis S. Nielsen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor researching how fermented foods affect gut microbiota and human health. His work intersects food microbiology with gut health research. Strong collaboration potential for bioinformatics and computational biology in microbiome analysis, metagenomics, and modeling host-microbe interactions.

name: Helene Christine Reinbach
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor and Head of Studies for MSc Food Innovation and Health. Research in public health, food innovation and consumer behavior. Limited direct relevance for computational biology but potential for data science applications in health outcomes research.

name: Annemarie Olsen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor in Sensory Science and Consumer Behaviour with research on food preferences, texture perception, and determinants of food consumption. Work involves understanding consumer behavior and sensory responses. Potential for collaboration in statistical modeling and machine learning for consumer data analysis.

name: Mahesha Manjunatha Poojary
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor in Ingredient and Dairy Technology, leading themes in the SEEDFOOD project on tailored modification of rapeseed proteins. Research focuses on protein functionality, enzyme-based modifications, and plant-based protein ingredients. Collaboration opportunities in computational protein design, bioinformatics analysis of protein properties, and enzyme engineering.

name: Ourania Gouseti
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor in Food Analytics and Biotechnology section, affiliated with Plant-based Foods and Biochemistry research group. Research on food structure and plant-based protein systems. Potential for collaboration in computational modeling of food structures and biophysical analysis.

name: Karsten Olsen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor in Design and Consumer Behavior section. Research focuses on consumer behavior and food design. Limited direct computational biology relevance but potential for data science and behavioral modeling applications.

name: Norbert Raak
title: Assistant Professor (Tenure Track)
summary: Assistant Professor (Tenure Track) in Ingredient and Dairy Technology. Leading multiple projects on plant-dairy hybrids, casein functionality from precision fermentation, and protein structure-function relationships. Strong potential for computational protein modeling, bioinformatics of protein modifications, and ML approaches to predict protein functionality.

name: Violetta Aru
title: Assistant Professor (Tenure Track)
summary: Assistant Professor (Tenure Track) in Food Analytics and Biotechnology. Research focuses on analytical chemistry, metabolomics and chemometrics. Good collaboration potential for bioinformatics, machine learning applications in metabolomics data analysis, and computational chemistry.

name: Qian Janice Wang
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor in Design and Consumer Behavior section working on sensory science and consumer behavior research. Focus on multisensory perception and food experience design. Potential for data science and computational modeling of sensory perception.

name: Henriette Lyng Røder
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor working on plant-based foods research. Part of the plant-based foods group focusing on sustainable protein sources. Collaboration potential in plant genomics, biotechnology applications, and computational approaches to protein engineering.

name: Jorge Ruiz Carrascal
title: Professor
summary: Professor with extensive research in meat science and technology, protein quality, and food processing. Author of over 100 papers in peer-reviewed journals. Limited direct relevance to computational biology and plant science, but potential for proteomics and food chemistry computational approaches.

name: Dan Staerk
title: Professor
topics: Drug discovery from plants and microorganisms
summary: Professor Staerk heads the Natural Products Research group and Copenhagen Small-Molecule NMR Center. His research focuses on discovery of small-molecule and peptide-based drug leads from natural sources including medicinal plants, marine organisms, and microorganisms. He develops analytical technologies for exploring chemical and pharmacological properties of natural products, with applications in diabetes and infectious diseases. Relevant for collaboration in plant science, bioinformatics for natural product discovery, and machine learning approaches to bioactivity prediction.

name: David E. Gloriam
title: Professor
summary: Professor Gloriam is a leader in computational receptor biology, specializing in G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs). His group develops GPCRdb, a major bioinformatics resource with >50,000 users. He combines data science, chemoinformatics, and computational chemistry in drug design. He uses AI methods and structural modeling, including AlphaFold2. Highly relevant for collaboration in bioinformatics, computational biology, AI/ML applications in drug discovery, database development, and genomics data integration.

name: Kristian Strømgaard
title: Professor
summary: Professor Strømgaard heads the Chemical Biology group (~20 people) focusing on peptide and protein engineering with therapeutic applications. His research includes targeting protein-protein interactions in brain diseases, biomolecular condensates via liquid-liquid phase separation, and receptor complexes. He founded Avilex Pharma and applies AI in drug discovery. Relevant for collaboration in computational structural biology, systems biology approaches to protein interactions, and AI-driven therapeutic design.

name: Bente Frølund
title: Professor
summary: Professor Frølund's research focuses on GABAA receptors and developing modulators for CNS disorders. Her work involves synthesis and pharmacological evaluation of compounds targeting brain receptors, with applications in stroke and neurodegeneration. She recently received DFF funding. Potentially relevant for collaborations involving computational modeling of receptor-ligand interactions and systems pharmacology approaches to brain disorders.

name: Anders Bach
title: Professor
summary: Professor Anders Bach (promoted August 2023) heads the Bach Group developing biological active small-molecule inhibitors of protein-protein interactions involved in oxidative stress, inflammation and brain diseases. His group works on fragment-based drug discovery. Relevant for computational chemistry collaborations, structure-based drug design, and possibly stress biology at the molecular level.

name: Rasmus Prætorius Clausen
title: Professor
summary: Professor Clausen (promoted August 2023) focuses on glutamate receptors, particularly NMDA receptors that are drug targets for brain disorders. His research involves developing chemical tool compounds to modify receptor function and targeting receptor subpopulations. He pursues new strategies involving artificial intelligence and sustainable methods in drug discovery. Relevant for AI/ML collaborations in drug design and computational approaches to receptor pharmacology.

name: Stephan Pless
title: Professor
summary: Professor Pless's lab investigates molecular function and pharmacology of ion channels - membrane proteins that regulate cellular excitability. His research involves protein engineering and discovering de novo cyclic peptides and microproteins. Ion channel dysfunction is associated with numerous diseases. Relevant for computational structural biology, protein design with ML, and systems biology approaches to understanding membrane protein function.

name: Lennart Bunch
title: Professor
summary: Professor Bunch focuses on organic chemistry and rational drug design, emphasizing how structural changes affect biological activity. His research involves synthetic chemistry to develop novel compounds targeting receptors. Recently received DFF funding. Less directly relevant for computational/bioinformatics collaboration but could benefit from computational chemistry support in structure-activity prediction.

name: Petrine Wellendorph
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor Wellendorph (Head of Drug Research Academy) specializes in molecular neuropharmacology focusing on GHB and GABA receptors/transporters in brain disorders. Her work includes deorphanization of GPCRs, cellular pharmacology, and generating knockout mice. She received Lundbeck Foundation Fellowships. Relevant for systems pharmacology collaborations and computational modeling of receptor mechanisms.

name: Anders A. Jensen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor Jensen works in molecular and cellular pharmacology with focus on receptor systems. Part of the Molecular Pharmacology discipline. Could benefit from computational approaches to receptor modeling and drug-receptor interaction prediction.

name: Hans Bräuner-Osborne
title: Professor
summary: Professor Bräuner-Osborne is highly cited (>15,900 citations) for work on molecular pharmacology and GPCRs. His research involves receptor characterization and cellular pharmacology. Relevant for computational receptor biology, systems pharmacology, and bioinformatics approaches to GPCR function.

name: Jette Kastrup
title: Professor
summary: Professor Kastrup is a leader in structural biology of ionotropic glutamate receptors (iGluRs), having determined >100 structures (~20% of all iGluR structures in PDB). Her group also works on GPCR structural studies in collaboration with Prof. Gloriam. She uses X-ray crystallography and cryo-EM. Interim Head of Pharmaceutical Informatics section. Highly relevant for structural bioinformatics, computational structural biology, protein structure analysis, and structure-based drug design using ML approaches.

name: Henrik Franzyk
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor Franzyk focuses on peptide-based/peptidomimetic antibacterials, SAR studies, bacterial delivery systems, and macromolecular drug delivery. His work involves solid-phase synthesis and developing antibiotics against multidrug-resistant bacteria. Could benefit from computational peptide design and ML approaches to predicting antimicrobial activity.

name: Paul R. Hansen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor Hansen works on peptide-based drug research including antimicrobial peptides and solid-phase synthesis. His research involves peptide chemistry and modification of drugs. Could benefit from computational approaches to peptide structure prediction and activity modeling.

name: Christian Adam Olsen
title: Professor
summary: Professor Olsen's lab focuses on chemical biology of histone deacetylases and post-translational modifications to lysine. His research involves developing inhibitors for SIRT enzymes and studying epigenetic mechanisms. Could benefit from computational approaches to protein-ligand interactions, systems biology analysis of epigenetic regulation, and bioinformatics analysis of modification patterns.

name: Michael Gajhede
title: Professor
summary: Professor Gajhede works in structural biology and protein crystallography. His expertise in 3D structure determination is relevant for structure-based drug design. Could collaborate on structural bioinformatics, computational structural analysis, and integrating structural data with ML approaches.

name: Osman Asghar Mirza
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor Mirza specializes in structural biology with expertise in determining protein structures using X-ray crystallography. Relevant for structural bioinformatics collaborations and computational structural biology applications.

name: Albert Jelke Kooistra
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor Kooistra specializes in data science for drug design. He recently received a major Carlsberg Foundation grant for 'Decoding Protein-Protein Dialogue Using ChatGPT'. His work focuses on drug-related data science and computational approaches. Highly relevant for collaboration in AI/ML applications, bioinformatics, computational biology, and using large language models for biological problems.

name: Umberto Maria Battisti
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor Battisti recently received DFF funding. Works in medicinal chemistry and drug design. Could benefit from computational chemistry and cheminformatics collaborations.

name: Anne-Marie Heegaard
title: Professor
summary: Professor Heegaard works in translational and clinical pharmacology. She received Carlsberg Foundation funding for in vivo imaging infrastructure. Her work bridges basic research and clinical applications. Could benefit from computational approaches to pharmacokinetics/pharmacodynamics modeling and systems pharmacology.

name: Jesper Mosolff Mathiesen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor Mathiesen works in molecular and cellular pharmacology. Part of the Molecular Pharmacology discipline group. Could benefit from computational modeling of cellular signaling systems.

name: Majid Sheykhzade
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor Sheykhzade focuses on cardiovascular and neurovascular pharmacology including hypertension, diabetes, obesity, and stroke. His research involves GPCR pharmacology, intracellular calcium handling and signaling pathways, and pharmacokinetics/pharmacodynamics studies. Could collaborate on systems pharmacology approaches and computational modeling of cardiovascular disease mechanisms.

name: Céline Galvagnion
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor Galvagnion-Büll works on protein aggregation in neurodegenerative diseases, particularly Parkinson's disease. Her research involves understanding how skin and brain cells relate to disease mechanisms. Could benefit from computational structural biology, systems biology approaches to protein misfolding, and bioinformatics analysis of aggregation-prone sequences.

name: Annette Langkilde
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor Langkilde works in structural biology with focus on peptides and proteins. Relevant for structural bioinformatics and computational structural biology collaborations.

name: Pablo Ivan Nikel
title: Professor
summary: Professor Nikel leads the Systems Environmental Microbiology group, focusing on building synthetic metabolism to make novel molecules in environmental bacteria. His research exploits Pseudomonas putida as a cell factory for producing organohalogens and expanding bio-production beyond Nature's boundaries. He develops CRISPR-based tools (pAblo·pCasso) for precise genome editing and engineers bacteria for sustainable chemical production.

name: Tilmann Weber
title: Professor
summary: Professor Weber leads the Natural Products Genome Mining group and is Associate Scientific Director. He pioneered the development of antiSMASH, the world-leading software for automated genome mining of biosynthetic gene clusters. His work combines genetic, biochemical, and bioinformatics methods for discovering and engineering natural product biosynthesis. Highly relevant for collaborators in bioinformatics, computational biology, and genomics.

name: Ivan Mijakovic
title: Professor
summary: Professor Mijakovic heads the Bacterial Signal Transduction group. His research focuses on understanding bacterial cell signaling, particularly post-translational modifications in Bacillus subtilis. He engineers bacteria for carbon capture through biomineralization and food protein production. His work combines omics approaches, experimental evolution, and systems biology for sustainable applications.

name: Morten Otto Alexander Sommer
title: Professor
summary: Professor Sommer is Scientific Director and leads the Bacterial Synthetic Biology group. His research focuses on microbiome therapeutics and microbial foods, studying how microbial systems establish, organize, and evolve. He developed metagenomic techniques for mapping antibiotic resistance and works on pan-genome analysis of lactic acid bacteria. Strong computational and systems biology component.

name: Bernhard Palsson
title: Professor
summary: Professor Palsson is Scientific Director at DTU Biosustain and a leading figure in systems biology. He pioneered genome-scale metabolic modeling, flux-balance analysis, and modal analysis. His research develops complete computational models of cells (E. coli, CHO cells, human pathogens) to understand and engineer metabolism. Highly relevant for computational biology and bioinformatics collaborators.

name: Lars Keld Nielsen
title: Professor
summary: Professor Nielsen is Scientific Director and leads the C1 Systems Biology group with Dr. Stefano Donati. His research focuses on microbes capable of growth on C1 substrates (CO2 and derivatives) for biotechnological upcycling. He develops computational models and experimental tools for engineering superior strains that convert C1 substrates into valuable products. Strong computational and modeling expertise.

name: Ditte Hededam Welner
title: Senior Researcher
summary: Senior Researcher Welner heads the Enzyme Engineering and Structural Biology group. Her research develops enzyme biocatalysts for industrial production of natural products (aromas, fragrances, dyes, pharmaceuticals). She combines structural biology, rational design, and machine learning for engineering glycosyltransferase enzymes. Her work includes life cycle assessment for sustainable enzymatic processes.

name: Joshua David Evans
title: Senior Researcher
summary: Senior Researcher Evans leads the Sustainable Food Innovation group. His transdisciplinary research combines culinary development, natural sciences, and social sciences to create sustainable foods. He uses DNA sequencing, metabolomics, and microbial ecology to study novel fermentations. His work bridges food culture, microbial evolution, and flavor chemistry.

name: Carlos G. Acevedo-Rocha
title: Senior Researcher
summary: Senior Researcher Acevedo-Rocha heads the Computational Protein Engineering (CPE) group. His research develops novel computational methods, including machine learning and AI, to understand and engineer enzymes and proteins for human and planetary health applications. He works on protein function optimization for converting waste like CO2 into value-added compounds. Strong AI/ML and computational focus.

name: Stefano Donati
title: Co-Principal Investigator and Senior Researcher
summary: Co-PI Donati co-leads the C1 Systems Biology group with Prof. Lars Keld Nielsen. His research focuses on systematic understanding of microbial metabolism for microbes growing on C1 substrates. He constructs extensive datasets and develops experimental tools for both natural and synthetic C1-metabolizing strains, aiming to engineer organisms for sustainable chemical production from CO2.

name: Alberto Santos Delgado
title: Director, Informatics Platform
summary: Director Santos Delgado leads the Informatics Platform at DTU Biosustain, establishing unified data infrastructure across research teams. He implements FAIR data principles and develops data management systems supporting AI/ML applications in biotechnology. His work connects bioinformatics with high-throughput biofoundry operations and supports data-driven strain design.

name: Lone Gram
title: Professor
summary: Professor Lone Gram leads the Center for Microbial Secondary Metabolites (CeMiSt), a DNRF Center of Excellence. Her research focuses on aquatic bacteria ecology and physiology, with goals to control disease-promoting bacteria and enable biotechnological applications. She investigates microbial secondary metabolites, their natural functions, and biotechnological applications, including work on marine biodiversity for bioprotectants in aquaculture and crop diseases.

name: Lars Jelsbak
title: Professor
summary: Professor Jelsbak works at the interface of microbial genomics, ecology, and functional genetics to engineer bacterial systems for medicine, sustainable agriculture, and biotechnology. His research focuses on plant beneficial microbes and microbiomes, with the goal of finding microbial solutions that support plant health and reduce agrochemical use. He also heads PhD education at DTU Bioengineering.

name: Alexander Kai Buell
title: Professor
summary: Full Professor of Protein Biophysics since 2019, Professor Buell investigates protein aggregation and amyloid formation. His group develops novel biophysical methods including Capflex for studying protein liquid-liquid phase separation. While focused on protein structure and misfolding rather than plant biology, his biophysical approaches and computational methods could be valuable for understanding protein behavior in biological systems.

name: Jane Wittrup Agger
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor Agger leads the Lignin Biotechnology group, focusing on enzyme discovery and development for sustainable plant biomass utilization. Her research involves enzymes for plant cell wall deconstruction, development of biophysical methodology, and enzymatic synthesis technology. This work is directly relevant to plant science, biomass conversion, and sustainable bioprocesses.

name: Susanne Brix Pedersen
title: Professor
summary: Professor Brix Pedersen is Head of Section for Protein Science and Biotherapeutics and leads the Disease Systems Immunology research group. Her work focuses on host-environment interactions, epithelial barriers, and immune system responses. While primarily focused on biomedicine and immunology, her expertise in protein science and systems biology approaches could be relevant for interdisciplinary collaborations.

name: Andreas Hougaard Laustsen-Kiel
title: Professor
summary: Professor Laustsen-Kiel leads the Section for Biologics Engineering, specializing in antibody discovery, protein engineering, and synthetic biology. His group works with monoclonal antibodies, nanobodies, and other binding proteins for disease combat, diagnostics, and biotechnology applications. The group has strong expertise in digital biotechnology and data analysis, with entrepreneurial focus having spun out several biotech companies.

name: Rasmus John Normand Frandsen
title: Professor
summary: Professor Frandsen is Head of Section for Synthetic Biology, leading the department's synthetic biology and cell factory engineering activities. His work includes gene editing tools for developing climate-resilient crops and alternatives to pesticides, genome sequencing of Aspergillus and Penicillium species. His research is highly relevant for plant biotechnology and sustainable agriculture.

name: Andrew Urquhart
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor Urquhart received DKK 9.1 million (with Timothy Jenkins) from the Lundbeck Foundation to support research in eye diseases and migraine. His work focuses on biomedical applications, though specific research details were limited in search results.

name: Timothy P. Jenkins
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor Jenkins is involved in the Data Science Hub at DTU Bioengineering, focusing on expanding the department's data science capacity. He received Lundbeck Foundation funding for eye disease and migraine research. His data science expertise makes him relevant for computational biology collaborations, particularly in bioinformatics applications.

name: Mikael Lenz Strube
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor Strube is involved in the Data Science Hub and Center for Microbial Secondary Metabolites. His research combines microbiome analysis with computational approaches, focusing on mining fungal and bacterial genomes for enzymes and secondary metabolites. His work spans soil and marine microbiomes with applications in biotechnology.

name: Mikkel Bentzon-Tilia
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor Bentzon-Tilia works in the Section for Microbial and Chemical Ecology and is part of the Center for Microbial Secondary Metabolites. His research includes engineering algal microbiomes for sustainable aquaculture and biotech, studying microbial communities in natural settings. His work is relevant for microbiome engineering and biotechnology applications.

name: Shengda Zhang
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Assistant Professor Zhang is part of the Bacterial Ecophysiology and Biotechnology group and CeMiSt. His research focuses on marine bacteria, particularly characterization and use of chitinases and biosynthetic pathways for marine bioactive compounds. His molecular bacteriology expertise contributes to understanding secondary metabolite production.

name: Anna Hammerich Thysen
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Assistant Professor Thysen received a 3.1M DKK Inge Lehmann grant for research on brain border type 2 immune cells in cognitive complications of allergic asthma. Her project investigates whether immune cells in the meninx are important for neurological complications. While focused on biomedical research, her systems-level approach to understanding immune responses could be relevant for interdisciplinary projects.

name: Birgitte Zeuner
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor Zeuner is a Villum Young Investigator 2024 with 7M DKK for the REGENZYME project on mastering regioselectivity in enzymatic synthesis. She also received DFF funding for LOOP-DESIGN: AI-based loop design for improved enzymatic synthesis. Her work combines enzyme technology with computational approaches including AI/ML for enzyme engineering.

name: Ákos T. Kovács
title: Professor
summary: Professor Kovács leads a research group in the Section for Microbial and Chemical Ecology. His work focuses on microbial community dynamics and interactions, though specific research details were limited in search results. His expertise in microbial ecology is relevant for understanding complex biological systems.

name: Ling Ding
title: Professor/Associate Professor
summary: Part of the Section for Microbial and Chemical Ecology research group. Limited specific information was available from the search results, but appears to be involved in microbial ecology research at the department.

name: Kedar Natarajan
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor Natarajan investigates cell-fate decisions in stem cells and CNS differentiation using patient-derived hiPSC. His group uses single-cell profiling including transcriptomics, ATAC-seq, multi-omics, and spatial transcriptomics, developing computational approaches for gene regulatory network identification. His expertise in computational biology, bioinformatics, and single-cell analysis makes him highly relevant for interdisciplinary collaborations.

name: Erwin Schoof
title: Associate Professor/Professor
summary: Dr. Schoof leads the Cell Diversity Lab, pioneering Mass Spectrometry-based single-cell proteomics. His team combines omics technologies with bioinformatics and functional validation to unravel biological concepts controlling heterogeneity in complex systems, especially blood system and cancer. His computational and multi-omics approaches are relevant for systems biology collaborations.

name: Anne S. Meyer
title: Professor
summary: Professor Meyer received DFF funding for Mechanoredox Biocatalysis - Powering Redox Enzymes with Vibrations. Her research focuses on enzyme technology and biocatalysis with innovative approaches. Her work in enzyme engineering and biotechnology applications is relevant for sustainable bioprocesses.

name: Jens Preben Morth
title: Professor/Associate Professor
summary: Received DFF funding for prospecting and harnessing the biotechnological potential of Amidase signature enzymes. His research involves structural biology approaches to understand and engineer enzymes for biotechnology applications.

name: Lars Behrendt
title: Assistant Professor/Associate Professor
summary: Received DFF funding for RhizoTrack: tracking microbial attraction and signal transformation in the rhizosphere. His research focuses on plant-microbe interactions in the root zone, which is highly relevant for plant science, stress biology, and sustainable agriculture. His work bridges microbiology and plant biology.

name: Rune Busk Damgaard
title: Assistant Professor/Associate Professor
summary: Received DFF funding for Unmasking Bacterial Tricks: sdAb Tools to Decode Host-Pathogen Interactions. His research uses antibody tools to understand molecular mechanisms of bacterial pathogenesis. While focused on medical microbiology, his protein engineering and molecular biology approaches could be relevant for broader biotechnology applications.

name: Bjarke Bak Christensen
title: Head of Department
summary: Head of Department at DTU Bioengineering, actively promoting gene editing technologies for developing climate-resilient crops and sustainable bioprocesses. He advocates for GMO and NGT (New Genomic Technologies) in biotechnology research and applications for sustainable agriculture and food security.

name: Stig Uggerhøj Andersen
title: Professor
summary: Stig Andersen studies natural variation in legume species (Lotus japonicus, white clover, faba bean) to understand genetic signatures of selection and dissect complex traits. His group uses extensive high-throughput sequencing and bioinformatics including genome assembly, variant calling, QTL/GWAS mapping, microbiome analysis, and single-cell RNA-seq. Major focus on legume-rhizobium symbiosis and crop improvement.

name: Simona Radutoiu
title: Professor
summary: Simona Radutoiu studies interactions between plants and soil microbes, focusing on signal exchange and recognition in binary and complex associations. Her research uses genetic and molecular tools to understand how plants discriminate beneficial from harmful microbes through LysM receptor-mediated signaling. Work spans from single microbes to complex soil microbiota, with applications to sustainable agriculture. EMBO member (2022).

name: Mikkel Heide Schierup
title: Professor
summary: Mikkel Schierup develops and applies computational methods for population genetics and genomics. His research includes primate evolutionary genomics and application of evolutionary principles to association mapping. Strong emphasis on statistical and algorithmic approaches to bioinformatics, with expertise in genome analysis, molecular evolution, and natural selection. Perfect for collaborations in evolutionary bioinformatics and statistical genomics.

name: Jens Stougaard
title: Professor
summary: Jens Stougaard investigates molecular genetics and mechanisms of rhizobial infection in legume roots, focusing on both intercellular and intracellular infection modes in Lotus japonicus. His group established the large LORE1 insertion mutant collection (130,000+ lines) available as a genetic resource. Former center leader of Centre of Excellence CARB. Partner in major international projects including ENSA. EMBO member.

name: Kasper Røjkjær Andersen
title: Professor
summary: Kasper Andersen studies the structural and functional basis for cell-surface receptor signaling in plant-microbe interactions. His research uses protein biochemistry, biophysics, and structural biology to understand molecular mechanisms of plant-microbe communication at atomic resolution, particularly focusing on how plants interpret microbes using receptors. Key contributor to understanding specificity determinants in LysM receptors.

name: Poul Nissen
title: Professor
summary: Poul Nissen is a leading structural biologist studying the structure and function of membrane proteins, particularly P-type ATPases including sodium-potassium pumps and calcium pumps. Vice-Dean for Research and recipient of the Anders Jahre Medical Prize (2021). His work advances understanding of diseases including cancer, cardiovascular disease and psychiatric disorders. Director of research environment but limited direct computational biology connection.

name: Torben Heick Jensen
title: Professor
summary: Torben Heick Jensen studies early transcription termination mechanisms and their interplay with RNA transport and degradation. His lab uses state-of-the-art transcriptome-wide sequencing and high-throughput mass spectrometry to uncover how cells sort genetic information. Chairman of Research Committee at MBG. Leader of Exo-Adapt research center funded by Novo Nordisk Foundation. Strong computational genomics component in RNA-seq analysis.

name: Ditlev E. Brodersen
title: Professor
summary: Ditlev Brodersen studies molecular mechanisms underlying microbial defense mechanisms in bacteria, viruses, and archaea using structural biology. Focus on toxin-antitoxin systems, CRISPR-Cas immunity, and specialized enzyme systems for antibiotic resistance. Chairman of PhD Committee at MBG. Primarily structural biology with less direct computational/bioinformatics focus.

name: Christian K. Damgaard
title: Associate Professor
summary: Christian Damgaard studies circular RNAs and their role in regulating neuronal differentiation and transcription. Representative from RNA Biology and Innovation section in the management team. Research focuses on RNA-mediated gene regulation mechanisms in human cells with implications for neurobiology and development.

name: Esben Lorentzen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Esben Lorentzen studies proteins and protein complexes responsible for intracellular transport that organizes eukaryotic cells. Representative from Protein Sciences section in management team. Recent work includes synaptic protein degradation as risk factor for psychiatric disorders. Primarily structural/biochemical focus with limited computational component.

name: Magnus Kjærgaard
title: Associate Professor
summary: Magnus Kjærgaard studies intrinsically disordered proteins (IDPs) using biophysical techniques including single-molecule fluorescence spectroscopy and NMR. Representative from Neurobiology section in management team. Focus on understanding protein structure and dynamics. Primarily experimental biophysics with potential for computational modeling collaborations.

name: Christian Storm Pedersen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Christian Storm Pedersen is representative from Bioinformatics Research Centre (BiRC) in the management team. BiRC focuses on developing computational methods for biological data with emphasis on molecular evolution, population genetics, and statistical/algorithmic approaches. Research spans from theoretical questions to program development and applications.

name: Thi Bich Luu
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Thi Bich Luu recently received funding from Danish Independent Research Fund. Her work includes research on conserved juxtamembrane motifs in plant NFR5 receptors essential for root nodule symbiosis, contributing to understanding of plant-microbe receptor signaling mechanisms.

name: Nikolaj Abel
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Nikolaj Abel is an Assistant Professor in the Plant Molecular Biology section. Part of the InRoot research program studying plant-microbe interactions and crop resilience. Works within the collaborative plant science group at Aarhus focusing on legume systems.

name: Yuya Hayashi
title: Associate Professor
summary: Yuya Hayashi works on zebrafish model systems, extracellular vesicle biology, and theranostic RNA origami. Representative from the group of assistant professors and non-tenure associated professors in the management team. Part of Cellular Health, Intervention, and Nutrition section (or RNA Biology and Innovation based on different sources).

name: Kasper Munch
title: Associate Professor
summary: Kasper Munch is an Associate Professor at the Bioinformatics Research Centre (BiRC). Works on computational and statistical methods for analyzing genomic data with focus on population genetics and evolutionary processes. Strong potential for computational biology and genomics collaborations.

name: Anders Krogh
title: Professor
summary: Professor Krogh is a leading bioinformatician known for pioneering work on hidden Markov models for biological sequences. He heads the Center for Health Data Science and works on deep generative models applied to gene expression and biomedical data. His research spans bioinformatics and machine learning theory and applications, making him an excellent collaborator for computational biology, genomics, and AI applications in plant science.

name: Christian Igel
title: Professor
summary: Full professor and director of the SCIENCE AI Centre, co-lead of the Pioneer Centre for Artificial Intelligence. His research includes deep neural networks, stochastic neural networks, and applications of machine learning to achieve sustainable development goals. He has worked on projects involving tree cover mapping in Africa and environmental applications, making him relevant for plant science and environmental genomics collaborations.

name: Yevgeny Seldin
title: Professor
summary: Professor and Head of the Machine Learning Section. His research focuses on theoretical foundations of machine learning. The ML section's work ranges from theory to applications in natural language processing, information retrieval, medical image analysis, and biological data modeling, providing strong theoretical foundations for computational biology collaborations.

name: Isabelle Augenstein
title: Professor
summary: Professor and Head of the Natural Language Processing Section, youngest female full professor appointed in Denmark (2022). Her research on NLP, multimodal learning, and explainable AI could be valuable for mining biological literature, analyzing omics data annotations, and developing interpretable models for biological applications.

name: Anders Søgaard
title: Professor
summary: Full Professor in the Natural Language Processing section. His expertise in core NLP and computational linguistics can support text mining of scientific literature, automated annotation systems, and knowledge extraction from biological databases.

name: Mikkel Thorup
title: Professor
summary: Professor and Head of the Algorithms and Complexity Section, VILLUM Investigator heading BARC (Basic Algorithms Research Copenhagen). ACM Fellow with expertise in fundamental algorithms and data structures. His work on efficient algorithms for massive datasets is relevant for analyzing large-scale genomic and biological datasets.

name: Rasmus Pagh
title: Professor
summary: Professor in Algorithms and Complexity section, ACM Fellow (2024), co-founder of BARC. His research on randomized algorithms and scalable similarity search is applicable to large-scale biological sequence analysis and comparative genomics studies.

name: Martin Elsman
title: Professor
summary: Professor and Head of the Programming Languages and Theory of Computation Section. His work on functional programming techniques for high-performance computing on GPUs and multicore systems can accelerate computational biology pipelines and bioinformatics algorithms.

name: Kenny Erleben
title: Professor
summary: Professor and Head of the Image Analysis, Computational Modelling and Geometry Section. His expertise in computational modeling and numerical optimization could be applied to modeling biological systems, protein dynamics, and cellular processes.

name: Kim Steenstrup Pedersen
title: Professor
summary: Professor in the Image Section. His research on image processing and computer vision, particularly medical imaging, can be applied to plant phenotyping, microscopy image analysis, and high-throughput imaging of biological samples.

name: Martin Lillholm
title: Professor
summary: Professor known for transforming research into commercial solutions in radiology. Co-founder of Cerebriu A/S and Biomediq A/S. His expertise in medical image analysis and AI could extend to plant imaging, phenotyping systems, and automated analysis of biological imagery.

name: Thomas Troels Hildebrandt
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor and Head of the Software, Data, People & Society Section. His work on data management systems and process modeling can support research data management, workflow automation, and collaborative platforms for computational biology research.

name: Panagiotis Karras
title: Professor
summary: Professor in the Software, Data, People & Society Section who joined DIKU in 2023. Recognized for designing robust, scalable methods for accessing, mining, analyzing, and exploring data. His expertise is valuable for managing and analyzing large-scale biological datasets and developing efficient query systems for genomic databases.

name: Omri Ross
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor in the Programming Languages section with expertise in financial technology and blockchain. While primarily focused on fintech, his work on distributed systems and smart contracts could potentially support data provenance and reproducibility systems in computational biology.

name: Christian Wulff-Nilsen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor in the Algorithms and Complexity section. His research on efficient algorithms for graphs, including planar graphs and dynamic connectivity, is applicable to biological network analysis, phylogenetic trees, and metabolic pathway analysis.

name: Dmitriy Traytel
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor in the Software, Data, People & Society section. His expertise in formal methods and verification could support the development of reliable and verified computational biology tools and ensuring correctness of bioinformatics algorithms.

name: Daniel Spikol
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor in the Machine Learning Section with focus on learning analytics and multimodal data. His expertise in analyzing complex multimodal data streams could be adapted to analyzing multi-omics data in biological research.

name: Desmond Elliott
title: Assistant Professor (Tenure Track)
summary: Tenure-Track Assistant Professor in the NLP section. His work on multimodal learning combining vision and language could be applied to integrating image data with textual annotations in biological research, such as linking microscopy images with genomic data.

name: Daniel Hershcovich
title: Assistant Professor (Tenure Track)
summary: Tenure-Track Assistant Professor in the NLP section specializing in semantic parsing and meaning representations. His work on cross-lingual NLP and semantic analysis can support automated literature mining and knowledge extraction from biological publications.
